attachment.content:
CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE — level: full

Respond terse like smart caveman. All technical substance stay. Only fluff die.

## Persistence

ACTIVE EVERY RESPONSE. No revert after many turns. No filler drift. Still active if unsure. Off only: "stop caveman" / "normal mode".

Default: **full**. Switch: `/caveman lite|full|ultra`.

## Rules

Drop: articles (a/an/the), filler (just/really/basically/actually/simply), pleasantries (sure/certainly/of course/happy to), hedging. Fragments OK. Short synonyms (big not extensive, fix not "implement a solution for"). Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged. Errors quoted exact.

Pattern: `[thing] [action] [reason]. [next step].`

Not: "Sure! I'd be happy to help you with that. The issue you're experiencing is likely caused by..."
Yes: "Bug in auth middleware. Token expiry check use `<` not `<=`. Fix:"

## Intensity

| Level | What change |
|-------|------------|
| **full** | Drop articles, fragments OK, short synonyms. Classic caveman |

Example — "Why React component re-render?"
- full: "New object ref each render. Inline object prop = new ref = re-render. Wrap in `useMemo`."

Example — "Explain database connection pooling."
- full: "Pool reuse open DB connections. No new connection per request. Skip handshake overhead."

## Auto-Clarity

Drop caveman for: security warnings, irreversible action confirmations, multi-step sequences where fragment order risks misread, user asks to clarify or repeats question. Resume caveman after clear part done.

Example — destructive op:
> **Warning:** This will permanently delete all rows in the `users` table and cannot be undone.
> ```sql
> DROP TABLE users;
> ```
> Caveman resume. Verify backup exist first.

## Boundaries

Code/commits/PRs: write normal. "stop caveman" or "normal mode": revert. Level persist until changed or session end.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- less-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- ship: Review all uncommitted changes, write a commit message, commit, and push — no co-author line.
- caveman:caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by speaking like caveman while keeping full technical accuracy. Supports intensity levels: lite, full (default), ultra, wenyan-lite, wenyan-full, wenyan-ultra. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman. Also auto-triggers when token efficiency is requested.
- caveman:caveman-commit: Ultra-compressed commit message generator. Cuts noise from commit messages while preserving intent and reasoning. Conventional Commits format. Subject ≤50 chars, body only when "why" isn't obvious. Use when user says "write a commit", "commit message", "generate commit", "/commit", or invokes /caveman-commit. Auto-triggers when staging changes.
- caveman:caveman-help: Quick-reference card for all caveman modes, skills, and commands. One-shot display, not a persistent mode. Trigger: /caveman-help, "caveman help", "what caveman commands", "how do I use caveman".
- caveman:caveman-review: Ultra-compressed code review comments. Cuts noise from PR feedback while preserving the actionable signal. Each comment is one line: location, problem, fix. Use when user says "review this PR", "code review", "review the diff", "/review", or invokes /caveman-review. Auto-triggers when reviewing pull requests.
- caveman:compress: Compress natural language memory files (CLAUDE.md, todos, preferences) into caveman format to save input tokens. Preserves all technical substance, code, URLs, and structure. Compressed version overwrites the original file. Human-readable backup saved as FILE.original.md. Trigger: /caveman:compress <filepath> or "compress memory file"
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
No tests at all. Yes — service should have tests. Most value:

1. **`mailer`** — unit test SMTP header injection guard (already fixed once per git log), template rendering, `To`/`From`/`Subject` fields
2. **`handlers`** — unit test each handler with a mock mailer: valid payload, missing fields, unknown event type
3. **`consumer`** — integration test queue binding + routing key `email.*` dispatch

Start with handlers + mailer (pure logic, no infra needed). Consumer test needs RabbitMQ so lower priority.

Want me to write them?

content.content:
1	package mailer
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"log"
6		"net/smtp"
7		"os"
8		"strings"
9	)
10	
11	const defaultFrom = "ThatCookiesMine <noreply@thatcookiesmine.dev>"
12	const defaultFromAddr = "noreply@thatcookiesmine.dev"
13	
14	// sanitizeHeader removes CRLF characters to prevent SMTP header injection attacks.
15	// An attacker controlling `to` or `subject` could inject additional headers or
16	// recipients by embedding \r\n sequences.
17	func sanitizeHeader(value string) string {
18		value = strings.ReplaceAll(value, "\r", "")
19		value = strings.ReplaceAll(value, "\n", "")
20		return value
21	}
22	
23	func SendMail(to, subject, html string) error {
24		host := getenv("SMTP_HOST", "mailhog")
25		port := getenv("SMTP_PORT", "1025")
26	
27		to = sanitizeHeader(to)
28		subject = sanitizeHeader(subject)
29	
30		msg := fmt.Sprintf(
31			"From: %s\r\nTo: %s\r\nSubject: %s\r\nMIME-Version: 1.0\r\nContent-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8\r\n\r\n%s",
32			defaultFrom, to, subject, html,
33		)
34	
35		if err := smtp.SendMail(host+":"+port, nil, defaultFromAddr, []string{to}, []byte(msg)); err != nil {
36			return err
37		}
38	
39		log.Printf("[mailer] Sent email to %s", to)
40		return nil
41	}
42	
43	func getenv(key, fallback string) string {
44		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
45			return v
46		}
47		return fallback
48	}
49

1 package handlers 2 3 import ( 4 "fmt" 5 6 "notification-service/internal/mailer" 7 ) 8 9 type EmailVerificationPayload struct { 10 To string `json:"to"` 11 Subject string `json:"subject"` 12 Data struct { 13 VerifyURL string `json:"verify_url"` 14 ExpiresIn string `json:"expires_in"` 15 } `json:"data"` 16 } 17 18 func HandleEmailVerification(payload EmailVerificationPayload) error { 19 html := fmt.Sprintf(` 20 21 22 23 31 32 33
34

Verify Your Email Address

35

Thanks for signing up! Click the button below to verify your email address and activate your account.

36 Verify Email 37

This link expires in %s. If you did not create an account, you can safely ignore this email.

38
39 ThatCookiesMine — Healthcare Appointment Platform 40
41
42 43 `, payload.Data.VerifyURL, payload.Data.ExpiresIn) 44 45 return mailer.SendMail(payload.To, payload.Subject, html) 46 } 47

1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"log"
5		"os"
6		"os/signal"
7		"syscall"
8		"time"
9	
10		"notification-service/internal/consumer"
11	)
12	
13	const (
14		retryDelay = 5 * time.Second
15		maxRetries = 10
16	)
17	
18	func start(attempt int) {
19		if attempt > maxRetries {
20			log.Fatalf("[app] Failed to start after %d attempts. Exiting.", maxRetries)
21		}
22	
23		if err := consumer.Start(); err != nil {
24			log.Printf("[app] Attempt %d failed, retrying in %s... %v", attempt, retryDelay, err)
25			time.Sleep(retryDelay)
26			start(attempt + 1)
27			return
28		}
29	
30		log.Println("[app] Notification service started")
31	}
32	
33	func main() {
34		quit := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
35		signal.Notify(quit, syscall.SIGTERM, syscall.SIGINT)
36	
37		go func() {
38			sig := <-quit
39			log.Printf("[app] Received %s, shutting down gracefully", sig)
40			os.Exit(0)
41		}()
42	
43		start(1)
44	}
45

content.text:
All 3 packages pass. Summary of what was done:

- **Refactor**: extracted `buildMessage` in mailer, introduced `handlers.MailSender` func type, updated handler signatures to accept it, added `mailSender` package var in consumer (overridable in tests)
- **`mailer` tests**: `sanitizeHeader` injection cases (CR, LF, CRLF), `buildMessage` header presence + RFC 2822 blank-line separation
- **`handlers` tests**: correct `to`/`subject` forwarded to sender, reset URL / verify URL / expires_in in HTML, error propagation
- **`consumer` tests**: routing for both event types, unknown event returns nil, malformed payload returns error, sender error propagates

Per the container-first rule in CLAUDE.md:

```bash
# from notification-service/
docker run --rm -v "$(pwd)":/app -w /app golang:1.23 go test ./...

# single package
docker run --rm -v "$(pwd)":/app -w /app golang:1.23 go test ./internal/handlers/...

# verbose
docker run --rm -v "$(pwd)":/app -w /app golang:1.23 go test -v ./...
```

message.content: this runs tests suit for notification-service ?

content.text: Yes. `./...` means "all packages recursively" — covers `mailer`, `handlers`, and `consumer` in one shot.

ok      notification-service/internal/mailer    0.002s
why does it do a download

lastPrompt: why does it do a download

content.text:
First run — no module cache in the container. Each `docker run --rm` starts fresh, cache gone on exit.

Fix: mount a cache volume so downloads persist across runs:

```bash
docker run --rm \
  -v "$(pwd)":/app \
  -v go-mod-cache:/root/go/pkg/mod \
  -w /app golang:1.23 go test ./...
```

After first run, subsequent runs skip the download.

message.content: it does not work like rspec ?

content.text:
Right. `rspec` runs inside a long-lived container (`docker-compose exec auth-api`) that already has gems installed. The Go container is ephemeral (`docker run --rm`), so no cache survives.

Two options:

**1. Add notification-service to docker-compose** (like auth-api) — then use `docker-compose exec`:
```bash
docker-compose exec notification-service go test ./...
```

**2. Keep ephemeral but persist cache** with the named volume from above.

Option 1 matches how the rest of the stack works. Want me to add it to docker-compose?

file.content:
# ThatCookiesMine — project helpers
# Infrastructure docker-compose wrapper
function dcinfrastructure
    docker compose -f ~/work/infrastructure/docker-compose.yml $argv
end

# Exec into a service container
function auth-exec
    dcinfrastructure exec auth-api $argv
end

# Auth API shortcuts
function auth-rspec
    auth-exec bundle exec rspec $argv
end

function auth-rubocop
    auth-exec bundle exec rubocop $argv
end

function auth-rails
    auth-exec bundle exec rails $argv
end

function auth-rswag
    auth-exec bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize
end

function auth-logs
    dcinfrastructure logs -f auth-api $argv
end

function notification-logs
    dcinfrastructure logs -f notification-service $argv
end

195	echo "  5. Copy selected text:              y (yanks to clipboard)"
196	echo "  6. Exit copy mode:                  Press Escape or Enter"
197	echo "  7. Paste:                           PREFIX + p or use your system paste"
198	echo ""

200	echo ""
201	echo "Current sessions:"

203	
204	# Handle session attachment

213	      tmux switch-client -t "$ATTACH_SESSION"

file.content:
#!/bin/bash

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Improved tmux repository manager script
# This script creates tmux sessions for multiple repositories with customized 
# windows and panes for development workflows
#
# Features:
# - Creates sessions for each repository with customized windows
# - Improved copy mode with vi keybindings
# - Mouse support for selecting and copying text
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Exit on error
set -e

# Default settings (can be overridden)
ATTACH_SESSION=""
SHOW_MENU=true

# Get the parent directory (where all repos are located)
PARENT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"

# List of repositories
repos=(
  "auth-api"
  "admin"
  "core-api"
  "frontend"
  "bruno"
  "infrastructure"
  "dev-setup"
  "mobile"
  "notification-service"
)

# Check if tmux is running
if ! command -v tmux > /dev/null 2>&1 || ! tmux info > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Starting tmux server..."
  tmux start-server
fi

# Detach from any existing tmux session if we're in one
if [[ -n "$TMUX" ]]; then
  tmux detach-client 2>/dev/null || true
fi

tmux new-session -d -s "general"

# Create a session for each repository
for i in "${!repos[@]}"; do
  repo="${repos[$i]}"
  repo_path="$PARENT_DIR/$repo"
  
  # Check if repository exists
  if [ -d "$repo_path" ]; then
    echo "Creating tmux session for $repo ($repo_type)..."
    
    # Check if session already exists
    if ! tmux has-session -t "$repo" 2>/dev/null; then
      # Create a new session for the repository with an initial window named 'code'
      tmux new-session -d -s "$repo" -c "$repo_path" -n "files"
      
      # Create additional windows based on repository type
      case "$repo" in
        "infrastructure"|"dev-setup"|"bruno"|"mobile")
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:files" "nvim" C-m
          ;;
        
        "auth-api")
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:files" "nvim" C-m
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "logs"
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:logs" "auth-logs auth-api" C-m
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "tests"
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:tests" "auth-rspec"
          ;;
        "core-api")
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:files" "nvim" C-m
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "logs"
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "tests"
          ;;
        "frontend")
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:files" "nvim" C-m
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "logs"
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "tests"
          ;;
        "admin")
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:files" "nvim" C-m
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "logs"
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "tests"
          ;;
        "notification-service")
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:files" "nvim" C-m
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "logs"
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:logs" "notification-logs notification-service" C-m
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "tests"
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:tests" "auth-rspec"
          ;;
        *)
      esac
      
      # Create a git window with lazygit for all repos
      tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "git"
      tmux send-keys -t "$repo:git" "lazygit" C-m

      # Create a general window for all repos
      tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "general"
      
      # Always go back to the first window
      tmux select-window -t "$repo:files"
      
      echo "✅ Session created for $repo with development windows"
    else
      echo "⏭️ Session for $repo already exists, skipping..."
    fi
  else
    echo "⚠️ Repository $repo not found at $repo_path"
  fi
done

# Function to show interactive menu
show_interactive_menu() {
  # Get list of sessions
  sessions=$(tmux list-sessions -F "#{session_name}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
  
  if [[ -z "$sessions" ]]; then
    echo "No tmux sessions found!"
    exit 1
  fi
  
  # Display menu
  echo ""
  echo "Select a session to attach to:"
  echo ""
  
  # Process sessions and display with numbers
  session_count=0
  while IFS= read -r session; do
    session_count=$((session_count+1))
    echo "  $session_count. $session"
    # Store session name in array
    session_array[$session_count]="$session"
  done <<< "$sessions"
  
  echo ""
  echo "  q. Exit without attaching"
  echo ""
  
  # Get user selection
  read -p "Enter selection [1-$session_count]: " selection
  
  # Handle selection
  if [[ "$selection" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && [ "$selection" -ge 1 ] && [ "$selection" -le "$session_count" ]; then
    selected_session="${session_array[$selection]}"
    echo "Attaching to session: $selected_session"
    
    # Check if we're already in a tmux session
    if [[ -n "$TMUX" ]]; then
      echo "Already in a tmux session, switching to $selected_session instead..."
      tmux switch-client -t "$selected_session"
    else
      # Not in a tmux session, so attach
      tmux attach-session -t "$selected_session"
    fi
    
    echo ""
    echo "📌 Remember: When inside tmux, you can switch sessions with PREFIX + s"
    echo "   (PREFIX is typically Ctrl+b)"
  elif [[ "$selection" == "q" ]]; then
    echo "Exiting without attaching to any session."
  else
    echo "Invalid selection. Exiting."
  fi
}

# List all created sessions
echo ""
echo "🎉 All sessions created! Use the following commands to interact with them:"
echo "  - tmux list-sessions                # List all sessions"
echo "  - tmux attach-session -t NAME       # Attach to a specific session"
echo "  - tmux switch-client -t NAME        # Switch to a specific session while already in tmux"
echo ""
echo "Once inside tmux, you can switch between sessions with:"
echo "  - PREFIX + s                        # Show session list and select (PREFIX is typically Ctrl+b)"
echo "  - PREFIX + (                        # Previous session"
echo "  - PREFIX + )                        # Next session"
echo "  - PREFIX + L                        # Last (previously used) session"
echo "  - PREFIX + d                        # Detach from current session"
echo ""
echo "To copy text in tmux:"
echo "  1. Enter copy mode:                 PREFIX + ["
echo "  2. Move cursor to start position    (use arrow keys or vi keys if enabled)"
echo "  3. Start selection:                 v (like vim) or space"
echo "  4. Move to end of selection         (use arrow keys or vi keys)"
echo "  5. Copy selected text:              y (yanks to clipboard)"
echo "  6. Exit copy mode:                  Press Escape or Enter"
echo "  7. Paste:                           PREFIX + p or use your system paste"
echo ""
echo "Mouse selection is also enabled - just select text with your mouse to copy"
echo ""
echo "Current sessions:"
tmux list-sessions

# Handle session attachment
if [[ -n "$ATTACH_SESSION" ]]; then
  if tmux has-session -t "$ATTACH_SESSION" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo ""
    echo "Attaching to session: $ATTACH_SESSION"
    
    # Check if already in a tmux session
    if [[ -n "$TMUX" ]]; then
      echo "Already in a tmux session, switching to $ATTACH_SESSION instead..."
      tmux switch-client -t "$ATTACH_SESSION"
    else
      # Not in a tmux session, so attach
      tmux attach-session -t "$ATTACH_SESSION"
    fi
    
    echo ""
    echo "📌 Remember: When inside tmux, you can switch sessions with PREFIX + s"
    echo "   (PREFIX is typically Ctrl+b)"
  else
    echo ""
    echo "⚠️ Session $ATTACH_SESSION not found!"
  fi
elif [[ "$SHOW_MENU" == true ]]; then
  show_interactive_menu
fi

#!/bin/bash

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Improved tmux repository manager script
# This script creates tmux sessions for multiple repositories with customized 
# windows and panes for development workflows
#
# Features:
# - Creates sessions for each repository with customized windows
# - Improved copy mode with vi keybindings
# - Mouse support for selecting and copying text
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Exit on error
set -e

# Default settings (can be overridden)
ATTACH_SESSION=""
SHOW_MENU=true

# Get the parent directory (where all repos are located)
PARENT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"

# List of repositories
repos=(
  "auth-api"
  "admin"
  "core-api"
  "frontend"
  "bruno"
  "infrastructure"
  "dev-setup"
  "mobile"
  "notification-service"
)

# Check if tmux is running
if ! command -v tmux > /dev/null 2>&1 || ! tmux info > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Starting tmux server..."
  tmux start-server
fi

# Detach from any existing tmux session if we're in one
if [[ -n "$TMUX" ]]; then
  tmux detach-client 2>/dev/null || true
fi

tmux new-session -d -s "general"

# Create a session for each repository
for i in "${!repos[@]}"; do
  repo="${repos[$i]}"
  repo_path="$PARENT_DIR/$repo"
  
  # Check if repository exists
  if [ -d "$repo_path" ]; then
    echo "Creating tmux session for $repo ($repo_type)..."
    
    # Check if session already exists
    if ! tmux has-session -t "$repo" 2>/dev/null; then
      # Create a new session for the repository with an initial window named 'code'
      tmux new-session -d -s "$repo" -c "$repo_path" -n "files"
      
      # Create additional windows based on repository type
      case "$repo" in
        "infrastructure"|"dev-setup"|"bruno"|"mobile")
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:files" "nvim" C-m
          ;;
        
        "auth-api")
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:files" "nvim" C-m
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "logs"
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:logs" "auth-logs auth-api" C-m
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "tests"
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:tests" "auth-rspec"
          ;;
        "core-api")
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:files" "nvim" C-m
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "logs"
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "tests"
          ;;
        "frontend")
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:files" "nvim" C-m
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "logs"
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "tests"
          ;;
        "admin")
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:files" "nvim" C-m
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "logs"
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "tests"
          ;;
        "notification-service")
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:files" "nvim" C-m
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "logs"
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:logs" "notification-logs notification-service" C-m
          tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "tests"
          tmux send-keys -t "$repo:tests" "auth-rspec"
          ;;
        *)
      esac
      
      # Create a git window with lazygit for all repos
      tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "git"
      tmux send-keys -t "$repo:git" "lazygit" C-m

      # Create a general window for all repos
      tmux new-window -t "$repo:" -c "$repo_path" -n "general"
      
      # Always go back to the first window
      tmux select-window -t "$repo:files"
      
      echo "✅ Session created for $repo with development windows"
    else
      echo "⏭️ Session for $repo already exists, skipping..."
    fi
  else
    echo "⚠️ Repository $repo not found at $repo_path"
  fi
done

# Function to show interactive menu
show_interactive_menu() {
  # Get list of sessions
  sessions=$(tmux list-sessions -F "#{session_name}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
  
  if [[ -z "$sessions" ]]; then
    echo "No tmux sessions found!"
    exit 1
  fi
  
  # Display menu
  echo ""
  echo "Select a session to attach to:"
  echo ""
  
  # Process sessions and display with numbers
  session_count=0
  while IFS= read -r session; do
    session_count=$((session_count+1))
    echo "  $session_count. $session"
    # Store session name in array
    session_array[$session_count]="$session"
  done <<< "$sessions"
  
  echo ""
  echo "  q. Exit without attaching"
  echo ""
  
  # Get user selection
  read -p "Enter selection [1-$session_count]: " selection
  
  # Handle selection
  if [[ "$selection" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && [ "$selection" -ge 1 ] && [ "$selection" -le "$session_count" ]; then
    selected_session="${session_array[$selection]}"
    echo "Attaching to session: $selected_session"
    
    # Check if we're already in a tmux session
    if [[ -n "$TMUX" ]]; then
      echo "Already in a tmux session, switching to $selected_session instead..."
      tmux switch-client -t "$selected_session"
    else
      # Not in a tmux session, so attach
      tmux attach-session -t "$selected_session"
    fi
    
    echo ""
    echo "📌 Remember: When inside tmux, you can switch sessions with PREFIX + s"
    echo "   (PREFIX is typically Ctrl+b)"
  elif [[ "$selection" == "q" ]]; then
    echo "Exiting without attaching to any session."
  else
    echo "Invalid selection. Exiting."
  fi
}

# List all created sessions
echo ""
echo "🎉 All sessions created! Use the following commands to interact with them:"
echo "  - tmux list-sessions                # List all sessions"
echo "  - tmux attach-session -t NAME       # Attach to a specific session"
echo "  - tmux switch-client -t NAME        # Switch to a specific session while already in tmux"
echo ""
echo "Once inside tmux, you can switch between sessions with:"
echo "  - PREFIX + s                        # Show session list and select (PREFIX is typically Ctrl+b)"
echo "  - PREFIX + (                        # Previous session"
echo "  - PREFIX + )                        # Next session"
echo "  - PREFIX + L                        # Last (previously used) session"
echo "  - PREFIX + d                        # Detach from current session"
echo ""
echo "To copy text in tmux:"
echo "  1. Enter copy mode:                 PREFIX + ["
echo "  2. Move cursor to start position    (use arrow keys or vi keys if enabled)"
echo "  3. Start selection:                 v (like vim) or space"
echo "  4. Move to end of selection         (use arrow keys or vi keys)"
echo "  5. Copy selected text:              y (yanks to clipboard)"
echo "  6. Exit copy mode:                  Press Escape or Enter"
echo "  7. Paste:                           PREFIX + p or use your system paste"
echo ""
echo "Mouse selection is also enabled - just select text with your mouse to copy"
echo ""
echo "Current sessions:"
tmux list-sessions

# Handle session attachment
if [[ -n "$ATTACH_SESSION" ]]; then
  if tmux has-session -t "$ATTACH_SESSION" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo ""
    echo "Attaching to session: $ATTACH_SESSION"
    
    # Check if already in a tmux session
    if [[ -n "$TMUX" ]]; then
      echo "Already in a tmux session, switching to $ATTACH_SESSION instead..."
      tmux switch-client -t "$ATTACH_SESSION"
    else
      # Not in a tmux session, so attach
      tmux attach-session -t "$ATTACH_SESSION"
    fi
    
    echo ""
    echo "📌 Remember: When inside tmux, you can switch sessions with PREFIX + s"
    echo "   (PREFIX is typically Ctrl+b)"
  else
    echo ""
    echo "⚠️ Session $ATTACH_SESSION not found!"
  fi
elif [[ "$SHOW_MENU" == true ]]; then
  show_interactive_menu
fi
